title: ClaraAnswers_RetellAgentSpec
description: Retell agent configuration spec. v1 from demo call, v2 from onboarding. Generated by agent_spec_generator_prompt from a validated Account Memo JSON.

account_id.description: Must match the account_id from the source Account Memo.

version.description: Must match the version of the Account Memo this was generated from.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this spec was generated.

source_memo_version.description: Version of the Account Memo used as input. Should equal version above.

agent_name.description: Human-readable label for this agent. Format: 'Clara - {company_name}'.

retell_config.description: Parameters passed directly to Retell agent creation API or entered manually in Retell UI.

voice_id.description: Retell voice identifier. Use a real Retell/ElevenLabs voice ID. This is what controls the actual voice — not tone_label from the memo.

voice_temperature.description: Controls expressiveness. 0 = flat and consistent, 1 = more varied.

enable_backchannel.description: Whether the agent uses backchanneling (mm-hmm, I see, etc.).

webhook_url.description: Retell webhook endpoint for call events. null if not yet configured.

max_call_duration_ms.description: Max call length in milliseconds. Default 10 minutes.

system_prompt.description: The fully rendered agent prompt. ALL {{VARIABLES}} must be substituted. Any unresolved variable must appear as [CONFIRM WITH CLIENT — field.path]. No blank variables. No markdown formatting — plain text only as Retell injects this directly.

not.description: Rejects any system_prompt that still contains unrendered {{VARIABLE}} tokens. Schema validation will fail if any raw template variable survives rendering. This catches partially-rendered prompts before they can be deployed.

key_variables.description: Snapshot of the critical runtime values used to render the system_prompt. Allows quick inspection without re-parsing the full prompt.

business_hours_description.description: Natural language. e.g. 'Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Central Time' or '[BUSINESS HOURS NOT YET CONFIRMED]'

callback_assurance_window.description: e.g. '15 minutes' if confirmed on onboarding, or '[UNCONFIRMED — must confirm before go-live]'

tool_placeholders.description: Tool invocations the agent can make. Never mentioned aloud to caller.

tool_name.description: Enumerated to prevent hallucinated tool names.

trigger.description: Condition that causes this tool to fire.

target.description: Phone number or destination for transfer tools.

note.description: Implementation note. Never shown to caller.

emergency_transfer_chain.description: Ordered transfer attempts for emergencies. Derived from escalation_chain in memo. order=1 is always tried first.

transfer_fail_action.description: What Clara does after exhausting chain.

trigger.description: Condition that triggers fallback. Always: 'all transfers in chain exhausted'.

action.description: Steps Clara takes: collect info, log, assure callback.

script.description: Exact verbatim words Clara says when fallback is triggered. No variables — must be fully rendered.

data_collection_fields.description: What data Clara collects per call scenario.

integration_constraints.description: Constraints rendered from memo. Agent must know these but never speak them aloud.

version_notes.description: Human-readable summary of this version. v1: list unconfirmed fields. v2: list what changed from v1.

questions_carried_forward.description: Fields still unresolved from the memo. Must be empty for go-live. Format: 'field.path — reason'. GO-LIVE GATE: The pipeline script MUST enforce that this array is empty before marking a spec as deployment-ready. A non-empty questions_carried_forward means the agent will contain [CONFIRM WITH CLIENT] placeholders in its prompt and MUST NOT go live. Schema does not enforce this mechanically (array length is unbounded by design for v1 drafts), but the pipeline script enforces it at the deployment-check step. See DESIGN.md §Go-Live Gate.
